summary: NQ and ES tagged fresh all-time highs at the open, then reversed and closed weak on the last business day of May. NQ printed ATH 30,535.75 on the RTH-open bar, reversed 266 points and closed 30,386.75 (+0.24%) — green but well off highs, with the 1hr ATR stop flipping overhead to 30,535. ES set a new ATH 7,611.75, faded to 7,590.5 (+0.03%) but held far better than NQ (stop still below at 7,581) — mega-cap tech distribution, not broad risk-off. Gold rallied with stocks: spiked 4,627.3, closed +0.94% at 4,569.7 (debasement/geopolitical bid). Oil stayed capped below $90, -0.69% to $87.80. Daily uptrend intact; intraday momentum stalled — likely month-end rebalancing. No first-tier scheduled release; month-end + post-PCE digestion.

Capital preservation day — both ACTIVE strategies OUT OF HABITAT. NQ gapped up ~500 pts to 29,700 then reversed hard to ~29,075–29,232. The Strait of Hormuz stalemate pushed oil higher (Brent ~$108, WTI ~$102), hot CPI reverberations persist, and chip stocks sold off. Powell's last week as Fed Chair adds uncertainty — FOMC minutes Wednesday expected to show historic dissent count. Gold failing to catch safe-haven bid despite equity weakness signals USD/rate pressure dominating. ES confirming NQ weakness with parallel move below ATR Stop and AVWAP. No trades today — wait for regime clarity post-FOMC.

regime: Risk-Off on Hot PCE (Gold Haven Bid Back, NQ Long Hangs On)
summary: Hot inflation flipped the tape risk-off and sent the haven bid back into gold. May core PCE printed +3.4% year over year, the highest since October 2023, with headline +4.1% and personal spending +0.7%, a firmer read that trims Fed-cut odds. Equities popped on the open then sold the rally all afternoon: MNQ closed 29,290 (-1.58%), hugging its 1hr trend stop just 30 points above the 29,260 line, while MES (-0.49%) flipped its stop long intraday but faded to close red and below 7,500. The split is the tell. Gold ran to fresh highs (+1.53%, ~4,104) as the inflation scare pulled the haven bid back, a mirror of last week's war-premium unwind, and crude fell -1.71%, flipping its 1hr stop short on growth worry. TSMOM-20 is technically still long but fragile into Jun 30 quarter-end. Trade the 29,260 line, not the story.

A hot GDP print sparked a hawkish open flush, then dip buyers took most of it back. The third estimate of Q1 GDP came in at 2.1% (versus 1.6% consensus) and jobless claims printed a tight 215K, a hawkish combination into tomorrow's PCE, so rate-sensitive MNQ flushed roughly 900 pts at the 9:30 ET open (30,215 to 29,296), broke its long stop intraday, then reclaimed the 1hr trend stop LONG at 29,258 and closed 29,760 (-1.12%) off the lows. MES (-0.58%) never confirmed - it held below its 7,454 stop all session, so the NQ-long / ES-short split is the caution flag. Commodities snapped back: crude +2.26% and gold +0.64% both flipped their 1hr stops long as the Iran risk-premium unwind paused. A two-sided whipsaw into Friday's PCE: trade the level, not the story.

regime: Risk-Premium Unwind (NQ Flips Long, Volatility Elevated)
summary: The Iran war premium left the tape, and the trade went into reverse. Oil supply fear eased hard (Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic resuming, US-Iran peace talks advancing, a 60-day US waiver on Iranian crude), so crude crashed -4.3% below 70 (its lowest since late February), gold lost its haven bid (-2.7%, breaking 4,000), and equities rallied on the disinflation relief. MNQ ran from a 29,263 low to close 30,099 (+1.17%), reclaiming 30,000 and flipping its 1hr ATR trend stop LONG at 29,549, the first long re-arm after two down days. MES (+0.4%) is pressing its 7,479 stop from below but has not confirmed. The clean decoupling (equities up, commodities down) is the opposite of Jun 23's everything-sold liquidation. Day-1 flip into a high-impact Thursday data open: trade the level, not the story.

Day 2 of the downtrend, and it broke wider. Equities extended Monday's TSMOM flip hard as an Asian chip crash (KOSPI -9.99%, Samsung and SK Hynix down more than 12%), a Broadcom guidance miss, and Fed late-2026 hike bets hammered the AI trade: MNQ closed 29,750 (-2.9%) below a falling 30,031 short stop and MES 7,446 below 7,482. The lone haven long broke too, with gold flipping short (4,129 below its 4,178.6 long stop), so all four HTA instruments are now short and even safety was sold. TSMOM-20 (long-only) stays flat and stands aside until a confirmed reclaim above 30,031.

regime: Trend Flip Down / Risk-Off Reversal (Volatility Re-Expanding)
summary: The post-FOMC, holiday-era uptrend broke today. On the first full session after Juneteenth, equities gapped to fresh highs overnight on a weekend US-Iran peace roadmap, then reversed hard at the cash open as the AI/semiconductor/momentum unwind reasserted. MNQ and MES both flipped TSMOM-20 SHORT at the 14:00 UTC bar (MNQ closed 30,662 below the new 30,863 stop; MES 7,540.75 below 7,582). Crude round-tripped a weekend supply-scare spike (78.50 to a 73.50 low) and flipped short on the 60-day Iranian-oil authorization, closing 74.05. Gold was the lone trend that held, firming its long to 4,209.8 above a rising 4,179 stop, a clean haven bid. ATR(14) re-expanded; volatility is back. TSMOM-20 (long-only) is now FLAT, stopped out of its long, and stands aside until a confirmed reclaim above 30,863.

regime: Holiday Drift / Uptrend Intact (Thin Juneteenth Tape)
summary: A quiet Juneteenth half-session that decided nothing and changed nothing. With US cash markets closed and CME equity futures shut at 1:00 PM ET, NQ and ES drifted in thin tape and held their post-FOMC repair: MNQ closed 30,651 (-0.22%) above its rising 30,461 ATR stop, MES 7,557 (-0.23%) above 7,526. TSMOM-20 stays armed long. Gold's 1hr stop flipped long on a marginal intraday reclaim, but it is net -1.30% on the day, a fragile thin-tape flip rather than a confirmed turn. Crude firmed +1.30% to 76.52. ATR(14) contracted again (MNQ 118.77 to 87.7); the only live risk now is the three-day-weekend gap into Monday June 22. Trade nothing you would not trade with a real read.

The first post-FOMC session faded the hawkish knee-jerk and reversed it in full. NQ reclaimed its ATR trend stop overnight and ground higher all day to close 30,726.5 (+1.94% on the clean September contract), with ES confirming (+0.85%) above its own reclaimed stop. VIX collapsed 11% to 16.40. Both indices flipped back LONG and TSMOM-20 is re-armed. The lone divergence is gold, which did not bounce, falling 1.13% to 4,228 and staying short. Note: today is the quarterly roll/expiry, so index levels are computed on the clean September contracts (the raw continuous print of +3.0% is a roll artifact). Throttle eases from DEFENSIVE toward REDUCED, but size stays capped into Friday's Juneteenth holiday and thin expiry tape.

The FOMC confirmed the break. A widely expected rate hold read hawkish once the dot plot and Chair Warsh's first press conference landed, and the 2:00 PM decision bar broke the morning range down across the board. NQ fell 0.51% to 29,832.75 (flushing to 29,612 before a late short-cover), ES lost 0.97% and the 7,500 line, and gold dropped 1.76% and LOST its 4,338 trend stop, so the last standing uptrend is gone. VIX jumped 12.37% to 18.44. All three major legs now sit below their ATR stops. Throttle stays DEFENSIVE into the first post-event session.

The uptrend snapped one day before the Fed. NQ reversed -1.79% off a fresh overnight high to close 29,985.75, below its 30,302 trend stop, and ES confirmed (-0.59%), also closing below its stop, so both index trend legs broke together. This was orderly de-risking, not panic: VIX rose only 1.30% to 16.41. Gold caught the defensive bid (+0.51%, holding its 4,338 stop), while crude crashed another -5.62%. Throttle steps to DEFENSIVE into Wednesday's 2:00 PM ET FOMC decision.

The trend extended into FOMC week. NQ ripped +2.88% off a weekend gap-up to fresh highs at 30,531.5, and ES confirmed step for step (+1.66%) to new highs, so this is broad risk-on, not a tech-only push. Both index ATR stops are trailing higher (MNQ 30,302, MES 7,530). VIX fell another 8.37% to 16.20, a third straight day of compression. Gold rose +2.15% but closed weak, below VWAP and near its rising stop. Crude was the lone risk-off tell, -3.74%, losing its ATR stop. Throttle is NORMAL today, stepping to REDUCED into Wednesday's FOMC decision (Jun 17, 2:00 PM ET).

The reclaim became a trend. NQ ground +0.83% on a news-free day and held every level it reclaimed in yesterday's PPI V-reversal; both index ATR trails are now RISING (uptrend confirmed). MES +0.52%, gold +0.15% holding its reversal, VIX -9.0% to 17.68 (second day of vol compression). Crude was the lone weak link, -2.42% to fresh lows. Throttle upgraded to NORMAL, with a plan to step down into FOMC (Jun 16-17).

regime: Trend Up Resuming — ES New ATH / Energy Shock Day
summary: New month, new high. ES printed a fresh ATH (7,632.75) on the first trading day of June. NQ swept lows at the open (30,295), reversed 397 pts to 30,692, and closed 30,523.75 (+0.45%). ATR trailing stops re-armed below price on both NQ and ES — regime flipped from Friday's BORDERLINE back to IN_HABITAT. The macro story: MCL surged +5.35% to $92.50 on a single shock candle at 13:00 UTC (OPEC/geopolitical), spiked to $94.79 RTH high then faded. Gold fell -1.19% to 4,515.4 — capital rotating out of gold into oil and equities. Strongest regime improvement since April ATH run.

Equities hold uptrend, gold breaks down — classic risk-on divergence. MNQ closed at 27,418 above ATR Stop (27,335) after absorbing a 244-pt overnight fade. MES printed fresh session highs at 7,211.50, closing above Friday. MGC broke below its ATR Stop and 4,700 psych — confirmed trend break on 1hr. VIX compressing at 18.02. Regime: UPTREND + NORMAL VOL + NO EVENT = core trend-following habitat. Clean Monday ahead of loaded macro week (GDP Thu, PCE Fri).

regime: Trend Recovering — ATR Stop Reclaimed (NQ + ES)
summary: Friday reversed Thursday's liquidation decisively — NQ rallied +499 pts to 27,441, reclaiming the ATR stop at 27,223 with a 218-point buffer. ES confirmed at 7,196 (above ATR stop 7,154). Volatility contracted 20% on NQ (ATR 14 from 109 → 87). The session was orderly — no afternoon flush, no bull trap. Seven consecutive green 1hr bars in RTH. Gold was the outlier: bounced from ON low 4,672 to RTH high 4,757, then faded to close at 4,724 — still range-bound below its ATR stop. This is ATR stop reclaim attempt #2 (Wednesday's reclaim failed Thursday). FOMC T-4 caps conviction. Stepping from PAUSED to DEFENSIVE (25%) IF Monday confirms with a 2-bar hold above ATR stop. No positions through FOMC Tue-Wed.

NQ1! grinding into the 26,010 zone after a constructive Tuesday session — bulls remain in control above the 1h ATR-trail at 25,892.76. ATR(14, 1h) prints 54.95 — squarely in the Normal volatility band per the ATR Regime Gate (no Shock signal, no Compression squeeze). Anchored VWAP from the 7/4 event sits at 25,314 (~696 pts below price), confirming buyers have controlled every reset since. The W20-3 Power Hour long bot exited at NY close (likely a green tag), and the TSMOM-style '1hr long 2.5 ATR stop' bot fired a fresh long into settle and is in-position — confirming the active stack is in habitat. Two MNQ1! 1h momentum bots currently in-position, one 'short 2 cons' bot armed but flat. No Tier-1 macro on the tape today; base-rate regime logic applies into Wednesday.
